enrichment.short_description: Softr is a no-code and AI-native platform for building business apps, client portals, and internal tools directly from data in Airtable, Google Sheets, HubSpot, and SQL databases.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Softr is an AI-native no-code platform for building business apps and client portals from your data. See pricing, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Softr is a no-code and AI-native application builder that lets businesses turn existing data from Airtable, Google Sheets, Notion, HubSpot, SQL databases, and 15-plus other sources into fully functional web apps, client portals, and internal tools without writing code. Founded in 2019 in Berlin, Germany by Mariam Hakobyan and Artur Mkrtchyan, Softr raised a 13.5 million dollar Series A led by FirstMark Capital and has grown into a widely used platform for startups, agencies, and internal-tools teams. Softr apps include user authentication, role-based permissions, workflows and automations, payments, and a professional, responsive UI that can be customized with custom code blocks. The platform offers a free plan supporting up to 10 users and 5,000 database records, alongside paid tiers: Basic at 49 dollars per month, Professional at 139 dollars per month, and Business at 269 dollars per month, plus a custom-priced Enterprise plan with SSO, SOC2 reporting, and audit logging. Higher tiers unlock more app users, AI credits, database records, custom user groups, and advanced data-source integrations such as HubSpot, BigQuery, PostgreSQL, and MySQL. Softr is best suited for teams that want to launch client portals, internal dashboards, marketplaces, or directories quickly, without hiring developers, while still allowing custom code for advanced needs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Softr
paragraphs: Softr is a no-code and AI-assisted platform for building business web applications directly on top of existing data sources such as Airtable, Google Sheets, Notion, HubSpot, and SQL databases. Instead of writing custom backend and frontend code, teams assemble pages, forms, and workflows using pre-built blocks and an AI co-builder that can generate an app from a text prompt.

Founded in 2019 in Berlin by Mariam Hakobyan and Artur Mkrtchyan, Softr has grown into one of the most widely used no-code platforms for client portals, internal tools, and directories. The company raised a Series A round led by FirstMark Capital and continues to add AI-native app generation and workflow automation features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Softr apps include built-in user authentication and role-based access control, so teams can restrict what different user groups can see or edit inside a portal or internal tool.

The platform connects to more than 15 data sources, including Airtable, Google Sheets, Notion, HubSpot, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and BigQuery on higher-tier plans, letting existing spreadsheets or databases power a fully functional app.

An AI co-builder can generate pages, layouts, and automations from natural-language prompts, and a library of pre-built blocks covers common patterns like directories, marketplaces, dashboards, and client portals.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Softr uses a freemium model. The Free plan supports unlimited apps with up to 10 users and 5,000 database records. Paid plans scale from Basic at 49 dollars per month up to Business at 269 dollars per month, with an Enterprise tier offering custom limits, SSO, and dedicated support.

Pricing is driven primarily by the number of app users, database records, and monthly AI credits and workflow actions included in each tier, rather than by the number of builders or collaborators, which are unlimited on every plan.

features.title: No-Code App Builder
features.description: Assemble pages, forms, and navigation using drag-and-drop blocks without writing frontend or backend code.

features.title: AI App Generation
features.description: Generate app pages, layouts, and workflow automations from a natural-language prompt using Softr's built-in AI co-builder.

features.title: Multiple Data Source Connections
features.description: Connect to Airtable, Google Sheets, Notion, HubSpot, PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, and other sources to power app content.

features.title: User Authentication and Permissions
features.description: Built-in login, sign-up, and role-based access control let you gate content behind custom user groups.

features.title: Workflow Automation
features.description: Trigger multi-step actions such as sending emails, updating records, or calling external APIs when app events occur.

features.title: Custom Code Support
features.description: Add custom CSS, JavaScript, and code blocks on paid plans for design or functionality beyond the standard block library.

features.title: Payments and Subscriptions
features.description: Accept one-time payments or recurring subscriptions inside an app using built-in Stripe integration.

features.title: Templates Library
features.description: Start from pre-built templates for client portals, internal tools, directories, and marketplaces to launch faster.

pros: Fast way to launch functional apps from existing spreadsheets or databases without hiring developers
AI co-builder speeds up initial app creation from a text prompt
Wide range of data source integrations, including Airtable, HubSpot, and SQL databases
Free plan is generous enough to fully test the platform before paying
Built-in authentication, permissions, and payments reduce the need for third-party tools
cons: Pricing scales quickly with app users and AI credits, which can get expensive for larger portals
Complex custom logic still requires writing custom code or workarounds
Performance can depend on the connected data source, especially with large Airtable bases
Some advanced data sources like BigQuery and PostgreSQL are limited to higher-tier plans

faqs.question: What is Softr used for
faqs.answer: Softr is used to build client portals, internal tools, directories, and marketplaces on top of existing data in Airtable, Google Sheets, Notion, or a SQL database, without writing code.

faqs.question: Is Softr free
faqs.answer: Yes, Softr offers a free plan that supports unlimited apps with up to 10 users and 5,000 database records. Paid plans start at 49 dollars per month for higher usage limits.

faqs.question: Who founded Softr
faqs.answer: Softr was founded in 2019 in Berlin, Germany by Mariam Hakobyan and Artur Mkrtchyan.

faqs.question: Does Softr require coding skills
faqs.answer: No, Softr is a no-code platform, though paid plans allow adding custom code blocks for advanced customization.

faqs.question: What data sources does Softr support
faqs.answer: Softr connects to Airtable, Google Sheets, Notion, HubSpot, PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, and more than 15 other data sources depending on the plan.

faqs.question: Can Softr apps have user logins
faqs.answer: Yes, Softr includes built-in user authentication and role-based permissions so you can control what different user groups can access.

faqs.question: Does Softr support payments
faqs.answer: Yes, Softr integrates with Stripe to accept one-time payments or recurring subscriptions inside an app.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Softr's Professional and Business plans
faqs.answer: The Business plan supports more app users, more AI credits and workflow actions, and adds data sources like HubSpot, BigQuery, PostgreSQL, and MySQL, while Professional is aimed at smaller portals and internal tools.

use_cases.title: Client Portals
use_cases.description: Agencies and service businesses give clients a branded portal to view project status, invoices, or shared files sourced from Airtable or a database.

use_cases.title: Internal Tools and Dashboards
use_cases.description: Operations teams build internal dashboards and admin tools on top of existing spreadsheets or databases without waiting on engineering resources.

use_cases.title: Directories and Marketplaces
use_cases.description: Startups launch searchable directories or two-sided marketplaces quickly using Softr's directory templates and built-in payments.

features: Advanced integrations like monday.com and Supabase

enrichment.short_description: SOGo is a free, open-source groupware and webmail server offering email, calendars, and contacts with CalDAV, CardDAV, and ActiveSync support for self-hosted collaboration.
enrichment.seo_title: SOGo: Open Source Groupware and Webmail Server
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SOGo is a free open-source groupware server with webmail, calendars, and contacts via CalDAV, CardDAV, and ActiveSync. See features and pricing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SOGo is a free and open-source groupware and webmail server designed for businesses and organizations that want to self-host email, calendaring, and contact management instead of relying on a hosted service. Its roots trace back to LSOffice in 1996 and the SKYRiX and OpenGroupware.org projects developed by Skyrix Software in Magdeburg, Germany; the current SOGo project began in 2004 as a scalability-focused fork. The Canadian company Inverse maintained SOGo for many years, and since 2022 the French company Alinto has served as its commercial steward, offering paid support and a hosted variant called SOGomail. SOGo is released under the GNU GPL and LGPL version 2 licenses, so the core server software is free to download, install, and modify, with costs limited to hosting infrastructure or optional commercial support contracts. It provides an AJAX-based web interface plus native client support through CalDAV, CardDAV, GroupDAV, and Microsoft ActiveSync, allowing synchronization with Thunderbird, Outlook, Apple Calendar and Contacts, and mobile devices. SOGo integrates with existing directory and mail infrastructure such as Active Directory, OpenLDAP, SQL databases, and IMAP servers, and it is used in production environments with thousands of mailboxes, most commonly by hosting providers, ISPs, and IT teams that need a self-hosted Exchange-like alternative.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SOGo
paragraphs: SOGo is a free and open-source groupware server that provides webmail, calendaring, and contact management for organizations that prefer to self-host their collaboration infrastructure instead of using a hosted email suite.

The project has a long history, tracing back to the SKYRiX and OpenGroupware.org groupware servers built by Skyrix Software in Magdeburg, Germany. The current SOGo project started in 2004 as a scalability-focused fork, was maintained by the Canadian company Inverse for many years, and has been commercially stewarded by the French company Alinto since 2022.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SOGo exposes calendars, address books, and mail through open standards including CalDAV, CardDAV, GroupDAV, and Microsoft ActiveSync, so it works with native clients like Thunderbird, Outlook, Apple Calendar and Contacts, and mobile devices on iOS and Android.

It reuses existing infrastructure such as Active Directory, OpenLDAP, SQL databases, and IMAP mail servers, which reduces migration effort for organizations that already run this infrastructure. Its AJAX-based web interface supports drag-and-drop, single sign-on via CAS, WebAuth, or Kerberos, and two-factor authentication.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SOGo's core server software is free and open source, released under the GNU GPL and LGPL version 2 licenses, so there is no license fee to install and run it on your own infrastructure. Organizations pay only for hosting and, optionally, commercial support contracts or a hosted variant such as Alinto's SOGomail.

Because pricing is not standardized like a typical SaaS product, actual cost depends on server infrastructure, in-house IT effort for maintenance and upgrades, and whether an organization purchases commercial support from Alinto or another provider.

features.title: Webmail and Groupware
features.description: Provides an AJAX-based web interface for email, calendars, address books, and tasks in a single self-hosted server.

features.title: CalDAV and CardDAV Support
features.description: Syncs calendars and contacts with native clients including Thunderbird, Apple Calendar and Contacts, and other CalDAV/CardDAV compatible apps.

features.title: Microsoft ActiveSync
features.description: Supports Microsoft Exchange ActiveSync so iOS, Android, and other mobile devices can sync mail, contacts, calendars, and tasks.

features.title: Directory Integration
features.description: Connects to Active Directory, OpenLDAP, and SQL databases for authentication and user management instead of requiring a separate user store.

features.title: Single Sign-On
features.description: Supports SSO via CAS, WebAuth, or Kerberos, along with two-factor authentication and configurable password policies.

features.title: Multi-Domain Hosting
features.description: Designed to scale to thousands of mailboxes and multiple domains, making it suitable for ISPs and hosting providers.

features.title: Outlook and Thunderbird Plugins
features.description: Offers a free connector plugin for Microsoft Outlook and native integration with Mozilla Thunderbird.

features.title: Open Standards
features.description: Built entirely on open protocols, avoiding vendor lock-in compared to proprietary groupware servers.

pros: Free and open source with no per-user licensing cost for the core server
Broad native client support through CalDAV, CardDAV, and ActiveSync
Integrates with existing LDAP, Active Directory, and IMAP infrastructure
Proven at scale in production environments with thousands of mailboxes
Active commercial steward (Alinto) available for organizations that want paid support
cons: Requires in-house IT expertise or a paid support contract to install, secure, and maintain
Web interface looks dated compared to modern SaaS email clients
No official cloud-hosted SaaS version directly from the core project, only third-party hosting
Documentation can be technical and geared toward system administrators rather than end users

faqs.question: Is SOGo free to use
faqs.answer: Yes, SOGo's core groupware server is free and open source under the GNU GPL and LGPL version 2 licenses. You only pay for hosting or optional commercial support.

faqs.question: What protocols does SOGo support
faqs.answer: SOGo supports CalDAV, CardDAV, GroupDAV, and Microsoft ActiveSync, allowing it to sync with Thunderbird, Outlook, Apple Calendar and Contacts, and mobile devices.

faqs.question: Who maintains SOGo
faqs.answer: SOGo is maintained by its open-source community, with the French company Alinto serving as commercial steward since 2022, following earlier stewardship by the Canadian company Inverse.

faqs.question: Can SOGo replace Microsoft Exchange
faqs.answer: SOGo is often used as a self-hosted alternative to Exchange for organizations that want webmail, calendaring, and mobile sync without an Exchange license, though feature parity varies by use case.

faqs.question: Does SOGo work with Outlook
faqs.answer: Yes, SOGo offers a free connector plugin for Microsoft Outlook in addition to native support for Thunderbird and CalDAV or CardDAV compatible clients.

faqs.question: What infrastructure does SOGo require
faqs.answer: SOGo typically runs alongside an existing IMAP mail server and can use Active Directory, OpenLDAP, or SQL databases for user accounts.

faqs.question: Is there a hosted version of SOGo
faqs.answer: The core SOGo project is self-hosted, but the commercial steward Alinto offers a hosted variant called SOGomail for organizations that do not want to manage their own server.

use_cases.title: Self-Hosted Business Email
use_cases.description: Small and mid-size businesses replace hosted email suites with a self-hosted SOGo server for full control over data and infrastructure.

use_cases.title: ISP and Hosting Provider Webmail
use_cases.description: Hosting providers deploy SOGo to offer webmail, calendar, and contact services to thousands of customer mailboxes across multiple domains.

use_cases.title: Exchange Alternative for Regulated Industries
use_cases.description: Organizations with data residency or compliance requirements use SOGo to keep email and calendar data on infrastructure they control.

features: Unlimited users limited only by your infrastructure
Community support via mailing lists and forums
Full access to CalDAV, CardDAV, and ActiveSync features
Access to the SOGomail hosted variant
Priority bug fixes and security patches

enrichment.short_description: Soldo is a spend management platform that combines prepaid company cards with expense automation, budgeting, and approval controls for businesses.
enrichment.seo_title: Soldo Pricing and Features: Spend Management Cards
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Soldo offers prepaid company cards with expense automation and budget controls. Compare Soldo pricing plans, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Soldo is a spend management platform that issues prepaid Mastercard company cards, both physical and virtual, paired with software for budgeting, expense automation, and approval workflows. Founded in 2015 and headquartered in London, United Kingdom, with additional offices in Milan, Rome, and Dublin, Soldo has raised more than 370 million dollars across multiple funding rounds, including a Series C, and employs over 350 people. The platform lets finance teams set spending rules and budgets per card, department, or project, automatically categorize expenses, capture receipts via mobile app and OCR, and sync transactions with accounting software. Soldo offers three main pricing tiers on its UK site: Standard at 21 pounds per month including 1 wallet and 3 cards, Plus at 33 pounds per month with multi-currency spending and OCR automation, and a custom-priced Unlimited plan with unlimited wallets, cards, and users plus SAP Concur integration and a dedicated professional services team. Soldo is aimed at small and mid-size businesses through larger enterprises that need to control employee and departmental spending without relying on manual expense reports or corporate credit cards, and it competes with products like Pleo, Payhawk, and Airbase in the European spend management market.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Soldo
paragraphs: Soldo is a spend management platform built around prepaid Mastercard company cards, giving finance teams control over who can spend money, how much, and on what, without relying on traditional expense reports or shared corporate credit cards.

Founded in 2015 and headquartered in London, United Kingdom, Soldo has grown into one of Europe's better-known fintech spend management providers, with additional offices in Milan, Rome, and Dublin and over 370 million dollars raised in funding.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Soldo issues both physical and virtual prepaid cards that can be assigned to employees, departments, or specific projects, each with its own spending rules, limits, and approval workflows set by finance administrators.

The platform automates expense capture with a mobile app, OCR receipt scanning, and automatic categorization, and it syncs transaction data with accounting software so finance teams can close the books faster without manual data entry.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Soldo's UK pricing has three tiers: Standard at 21 pounds per month for 1 wallet, 3 users, and 3 cards; Plus at 33 pounds per month adding multi-currency spending and OCR automation; and Unlimited, a custom-priced plan for larger organizations with unlimited wallets, users, and cards plus SAP Concur integration.
Additional card issuance fees apply, including a one-time fee per physical card and a smaller fee per virtual card. Both Standard and Plus plans include a 30-day free trial.

features.title: Prepaid Company Cards
features.description: Issue physical and virtual Mastercard prepaid cards to employees, departments, or projects with individual spending limits.

features.title: Spend Controls and Rules
features.description: Set budgets, category restrictions, and approval workflows per card or wallet to prevent overspending before it happens.

features.title: Receipt Capture and OCR
features.description: Employees capture receipts via mobile app, with OCR automatically extracting expense data to reduce manual entry.

features.title: Multi-Currency Spending
features.description: Plus and Unlimited plans support spending in multiple currencies, useful for businesses with international travel or teams.

features.title: Accounting Integrations
features.description: Sync transactions and expense data with accounting software to speed up reconciliation and month-end close.

features.title: Multiple Wallets
features.description: Create separate wallets for departments, projects, or cost centers to keep budgets and spending visible and separated.

features.title: Amazon Business and SAP Concur Integration
features.description: Connect Soldo to Amazon Business on the Plus plan or SAP Concur on the Unlimited plan for procurement and expense workflows.

features.title: Role-Based Access Control
features.description: Assign granular permissions so administrators, approvers, and cardholders each see and control only what they need.

pros: Combines company cards and expense software into a single platform, reducing manual expense reports
Granular spending controls help prevent budget overruns before transactions happen
Multi-currency support and OCR receipt capture are useful for international or high-volume spending teams
Backed by significant funding and an established presence in the UK and European markets
30-day free trial available on the Standard and Plus plans
cons: Pricing is per plan plus per-card issuance fees, which can add up for larger card fleets
Unlimited plan requires a sales demo rather than transparent self-serve pricing
Primarily focused on the UK and European markets, which may limit fit for US-only companies
Some advanced integrations like SAP Concur are limited to the top-tier plan

faqs.question: What is Soldo used for
faqs.answer: Soldo is used by finance teams to issue prepaid company cards and control employee, departmental, or project spending with built-in budgeting and expense automation.

faqs.question: How much does Soldo cost
faqs.answer: Soldo's Standard plan starts at 21 pounds per month, Plus is 33 pounds per month, and the Unlimited plan uses custom pricing based on company size and needs.

faqs.question: When was Soldo founded
faqs.answer: Soldo was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in London, United Kingdom.

faqs.question: Does Soldo offer a free trial
faqs.answer: Yes, Soldo offers a 30-day free trial on its Standard and Plus plans.

faqs.question: Does Soldo support multiple currencies
faqs.answer: Yes, multi-currency spending is available starting on the Plus plan.

faqs.question: What accounting software does Soldo integrate with
faqs.answer: Soldo integrates with popular accounting software for transaction syncing and reconciliation, with more advanced integrations like SAP Concur on the Unlimited plan.

faqs.question: Are Soldo cards physical or virtual
faqs.answer: Soldo offers both physical prepaid Mastercards and virtual cards, each with its own issuance fee.

faqs.question: How many employees does Soldo have
faqs.answer: Soldo has more than 350 employees across offices in London, Milan, Rome, and Dublin.

use_cases.title: Departmental Budget Control
use_cases.description: Finance teams issue cards to each department with preset budgets so spending stays within limits without manual approval chains for every purchase.

use_cases.title: Employee Expense Cards
use_cases.description: Businesses replace expense reports and reimbursements with prepaid cards that automatically capture receipts and categorize spend.

use_cases.title: Project and Client Spend Tracking
use_cases.description: Agencies and project-based businesses use separate wallets per client or project to track and bill back spend accurately.

enrichment.short_description: SOLECTRUS is a free, open-source, self-hosted dashboard for monitoring solar PV production, consumption, battery usage, grid exchange, and savings.
enrichment.seo_title: SOLECTRUS: Open Source Solar Monitoring Dashboard
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SOLECTRUS is a free, self-hosted dashboard for monitoring solar production, consumption, battery, and grid data. See features and setup options.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SOLECTRUS is a free and open-source, self-hosted dashboard for monitoring photovoltaic solar systems, tracking production, household consumption, battery charge and discharge, grid import and export, and the financial savings a solar installation generates. Created and maintained by German developer Georg Ledermann starting around 2020, the project is released under the AGPL-3.0 open-source license, though the SOLECTRUS name and logo remain trademarks of its creator. The software is designed to run on a Raspberry Pi or any Linux server, typically alongside a time-series database, and users can generate a ready-to-use Docker configuration through an online configurator at configurator.solectrus.de. SOLECTRUS pulls data from common inverter and energy-monitoring hardware and presents it in real-time and historical dashboards, letting solar system owners see at a glance how much energy they are producing, using, storing, and feeding back to the grid, along with automatically calculated cost savings. The project also offers a Model Context Protocol server, allowing AI assistants such as Claude to answer natural-language questions about a user's solar data. SOLECTRUS is aimed at residential and small commercial solar system owners, and hobbyists who want detailed, self-hosted visibility into their energy system without relying on a manufacturer's proprietary cloud app.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SOLECTRUS
paragraphs: SOLECTRUS is a free, open-source dashboard that gives solar photovoltaic system owners real-time and historical visibility into how much energy they produce, consume, store in a battery, and exchange with the grid, along with automatically calculated cost savings.

The project is created and maintained by German developer Georg Ledermann, with active development since around 2020. It is self-hosted, typically running on a Raspberry Pi or a Linux server, and released under the AGPL-3.0 open-source license.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SOLECTRUS visualizes solar production, household consumption, battery state of charge, and grid import and export in configurable dashboards, and it automatically calculates the financial savings generated by a solar installation over time.

The software integrates with common inverter and energy-monitoring hardware, and it can be deployed using an online configurator that generates a ready-to-use Docker setup, simplifying installation for users comfortable with self-hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SOLECTRUS is free and open source under the AGPL-3.0 license, so there is no subscription fee to download, install, and run it. Users are responsible for their own hosting hardware, such as a Raspberry Pi or small server, and any associated electricity or infrastructure costs.
Because SOLECTRUS is self-hosted rather than a managed SaaS product, there are no tiered subscription plans; the software itself is free, and the only ongoing cost is the infrastructure it runs on.

features.title: Real-Time Solar Dashboard
features.description: View live production, consumption, battery, and grid data in a configurable, self-hosted dashboard.

features.title: Historical Data and Trends
features.description: Track production and consumption trends over days, months, and years to understand system performance.

features.title: Financial Savings Calculation
features.description: Automatically calculates the money saved and earned from self-consumption, battery use, and grid feed-in.

features.title: Battery Monitoring
features.description: Displays battery charge level, charge and discharge rates, and state of health for hybrid solar and storage systems.

features.title: Self-Hosted and Open Source
features.description: Runs entirely on your own infrastructure, with full source code available under the AGPL-3.0 license.

features.title: Docker Configurator
features.description: Generates a ready-to-use Docker Compose configuration through an online configurator for simplified setup.

features.title: Hardware Integrations
features.description: Connects to common inverters and energy-monitoring hardware to pull production and consumption data.

features.title: AI and MCP Support
features.description: Exposes solar data through a Model Context Protocol server so AI assistants can answer natural-language questions about system performance.

pros: Completely free and open source with no subscription fees
Full data ownership since the dashboard is self-hosted rather than tied to a manufacturer's cloud app
Detailed financial savings calculations help quantify the value of a solar investment
Active development and a configurator that simplifies Docker-based deployment
cons: Requires self-hosting skills such as running a Raspberry Pi or Linux server and Docker
Limited to users whose inverter or monitoring hardware is supported
No official managed cloud hosting option directly from the project
Smaller community and documentation base compared to large commercial monitoring platforms

faqs.question: Is SOLECTRUS free
faqs.answer: Yes, SOLECTRUS is free and open source under the AGPL-3.0 license. You only need your own hosting hardware, such as a Raspberry Pi or Linux server.

faqs.question: Who created SOLECTRUS
faqs.answer: SOLECTRUS was created and is maintained by German developer Georg Ledermann, with development ongoing since around 2020.

faqs.question: What hardware do I need to run SOLECTRUS
faqs.answer: SOLECTRUS is typically self-hosted on a Raspberry Pi or any Linux server capable of running Docker, alongside a time-series database.

faqs.question: Does SOLECTRUS support battery systems
faqs.answer: Yes, SOLECTRUS can monitor battery state of charge, charge and discharge rates, and overall storage system performance.

faqs.question: Can SOLECTRUS calculate savings from my solar system
faqs.answer: Yes, it automatically calculates financial savings and earnings based on self-consumption, battery usage, and grid feed-in.

faqs.question: Is SOLECTRUS a cloud service
faqs.answer: No, SOLECTRUS is self-hosted software. There is no official managed cloud version from the project itself.

faqs.question: Does SOLECTRUS work with AI assistants
faqs.answer: Yes, SOLECTRUS offers a Model Context Protocol server so AI assistants like Claude can answer natural-language questions about your solar data.

use_cases.title: Residential Solar Monitoring
use_cases.description: Homeowners with rooftop solar and battery storage self-host SOLECTRUS to track production, consumption, and savings in real time.

use_cases.title: Small Commercial PV Systems
use_cases.description: Small businesses with on-site solar installations use SOLECTRUS to monitor system performance without a manufacturer subscription.

use_cases.title: Solar Hobbyist Dashboards
use_cases.description: Hobbyists and home automation enthusiasts integrate SOLECTRUS into their self-hosted smart home dashboards for detailed energy insights.

features: Unlimited historical data retention, subject to your own storage

enrichment.short_description: Solidtime is an open-source time tracking app for freelancers and teams, available as a free self-hosted deployment or a paid managed cloud plan.
enrichment.seo_title: Solidtime: Open Source Time Tracking Pricing
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Solidtime is an open-source time tracking app for freelancers and teams. Compare free self-hosted and paid cloud pricing plans and features.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Solidtime is an open-source time tracking application built for freelancers, agencies, and teams that want a modern alternative to expensive, closed-source SaaS time trackers such as Toggl or Harvest. Founded around 2024 and based in Vienna, Austria, Solidtime is released under the AGPL-3.0 license, with the full source code available on GitHub, meaning it can be self-hosted for free indefinitely. The company also offers a managed cloud version with a Solo plan that is free for individual freelancers and a Professional plan priced at 8 euros per user per month when billed annually, or 9 euros per user per month billed monthly. The Professional plan adds team access management, invoicing, project and task estimates, time entry rounding, and PDF or shareable reports. Core features available across plans include time entry tracking, clients, projects, tags, tasks, billable hours and rates, and reporting. A separate on-premise pricing structure exists for organizations that want to self-host with a commercial license and support beyond the free open-source option. Solidtime is best suited for freelancers who bill clients by the hour, small agencies managing multiple client projects, and privacy-conscious teams that prefer to self-host rather than rely on a third-party SaaS provider.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Solidtime
paragraphs: Solidtime is an open-source time tracking application designed for freelancers and agencies that need to track billable hours, manage clients and projects, and generate reports, without paying for a closed-source SaaS subscription.

Founded around 2024 and based in Vienna, Austria, Solidtime is released under the AGPL-3.0 license with its full source code on GitHub. It can be self-hosted for free indefinitely, or used through Solidtime's managed cloud offering.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Solidtime tracks time entries against clients, projects, tags, and tasks, with support for billable rates so freelancers and agencies can invoice clients accurately based on logged hours.

The Professional plan adds team-level access management at both the project and organization level, invoicing, project and task estimates, time entry rounding, and exportable PDF or shareable reports, making it suitable for small agencies as well as solo freelancers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Solidtime's cloud pricing has two tiers: Solo, which is free and aimed at freelancers tracking their own time, and Professional, priced at 8 euros per user per month billed annually or 9 euros per user per month billed monthly, aimed at teams that bill clients and need access control.

Because Solidtime is open source, self-hosting the software remains free indefinitely under the AGPL-3.0 license. A separate on-premise pricing structure covers organizations that want commercial licensing terms or support beyond the free self-hosted option.

features.title: Time Entry Tracking
features.description: Log billable and non-billable time against clients, projects, and tasks with a simple, modern interface.

features.title: Client and Project Management
features.description: Organize work by client and project, with tags for further categorization and filtering.

features.title: Billable Rates
features.description: Set billable rates per client, project, or user to generate accurate invoices from tracked time.

features.title: Team Access Management
features.description: Manage roles and permissions at the project and organization level on the Professional plan.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Generate invoices directly from tracked billable time on the Professional plan.

features.title: Reporting and Exports
features.description: Create PDF and shareable reports to summarize time spent by client, project, or team member.

features.title: Self-Hosting
features.description: Deploy Solidtime on your own infrastructure for free using the open-source AGPL-3.0 licensed code.

features.title: Project and Task Estimates
features.description: Set time estimates for projects and tasks to track progress against budgeted hours on the Professional plan.

pros: Free and open source with the option to self-host indefinitely at no cost
Modern, clean interface compared to many legacy time tracking tools
Transparent, low-cost paid tier for teams that want a managed cloud version
Built-in invoicing and billable rate tracking suited to freelancers and agencies
cons: Newer project, founded around 2024, with a smaller feature set and community than established competitors like Toggl or Harvest
Self-hosting requires technical setup and ongoing maintenance
Fewer third-party integrations compared to mature commercial time trackers
Company size and long-term backing are not well documented, which may concern risk-averse enterprise buyers

faqs.question: Is Solidtime free
faqs.answer: Yes, Solidtime offers a free Solo plan for individual freelancers, and the software can be self-hosted for free indefinitely under its AGPL-3.0 open-source license.

faqs.question: How much does Solidtime cost
faqs.answer: Solidtime's Professional cloud plan costs 8 euros per user per month billed annually, or 9 euros per user per month billed monthly.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Solidtime
faqs.answer: Yes, Solidtime's full source code is available on GitHub under the AGPL-3.0 license, and it can be self-hosted for free.

faqs.question: Who is Solidtime for
faqs.answer: Solidtime is built for freelancers and agencies that need to track billable hours, manage clients and projects, and generate invoices and reports.

faqs.question: Where is Solidtime based
faqs.answer: Solidtime is based in Vienna, Austria.

faqs.question: Does Solidtime support invoicing
faqs.answer: Yes, invoicing is available on the Professional plan, generated directly from tracked billable time.

faqs.question: What license does Solidtime use
faqs.answer: Solidtime is released under the GNU Affero General Public License version 3, AGPL-3.0.

use_cases.title: Freelancer Time and Billing
use_cases.description: Freelancers track hours per client and project and use billable rates to prepare accurate invoices.

use_cases.title: Agency Client Time Tracking
use_cases.description: Small agencies track time across multiple client projects with role-based access for team members.

use_cases.title: Self-Hosted Time Tracking
use_cases.description: Privacy-conscious teams self-host Solidtime on their own infrastructure to keep time and client data in-house.

features: Team access management at project and organization level
Unlimited users on your own infrastructure

enrichment.short_description: Solidus is a free, open-source ecommerce framework built on Ruby on Rails, forked from Spree, used by direct-to-consumer brands for fully customizable online stores.
enrichment.seo_title: Solidus: Open Source Ruby on Rails Ecommerce
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Solidus is a free, open-source ecommerce platform built on Ruby on Rails. Learn about features, governance, pricing, and who uses Solidus.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Solidus is a free, open-source ecommerce framework built on Ruby on Rails, created in 2015 when the consultancy Stembolt forked Spree to prioritize stability, backward compatibility, and easier upgrades. After Stembolt was acquired by JUUL Labs in 2018, the Solidus community reorganized under a decentralized governance model, with the Italian software agency Nebulab serving as the largest code contributor and a rotating group of Project Directors and a Core Team overseeing the project's technical and business direction. Because Solidus is a Rails engine rather than a hosted product, it is entirely free to use, and there is no vendor-set subscription price; costs come from hosting, developer time, and optional commercial support purchased from agencies like Nebulab. Solidus ships with a functional backend out of the box, covering products, orders, payments, promotions, and shipping, while being designed for deep customization through Rails' native override mechanisms and built-in extensions. It has been used in production by well-known direct-to-consumer brands including Bonobos, Casper, Away, MeUndies, and Ace and Tate. Solidus is best suited for engineering teams that want full control over their ecommerce codebase and are comfortable working in Ruby on Rails, rather than businesses looking for a no-code or fully managed SaaS storefront.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Solidus
paragraphs: Solidus is a free, open-source ecommerce framework built on Ruby on Rails. It was created in 2015 as a fork of Spree by the consultancy Stembolt, which wanted a more stable, backward-compatible foundation for the online store it was building for the fashion brand Bonobos.

After Stembolt was acquired in 2018, the Solidus community reorganized under a decentralized governance model. The Italian software agency Nebulab is the largest code contributor today, and the project is overseen by a rotating group of Project Directors and a technical Core Team rather than a single company.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Solidus is structured as a Rails engine, giving developers a fully functional ecommerce backend covering products, orders, payments, promotions, and shipping, while allowing every part of the platform to be customized or overridden using Rails' native mechanisms.

It has been used in production by numerous direct-to-consumer brands, including Bonobos, Casper, Away, MeUndies, FLOYD, Maisonette, and Ace and Tate, demonstrating its ability to handle real-world, high-traffic ecommerce workloads.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Solidus itself is completely free and open source; there is no license fee to download, install, or modify the codebase. As a self-hosted Rails application, the actual cost of running a Solidus store comes from developer time, hosting infrastructure, and any third-party extensions or services you choose to integrate.

Organizations that want dedicated implementation help or ongoing support typically hire an agency such as Nebulab or another Solidus partner, and those engagements are priced individually rather than through a fixed subscription.

features.title: Open Source Rails Engine
features.description: Solidus ships as a Ruby on Rails engine, giving developers full access to the source code and the ability to override any component.

features.title: Order and Payment Management
features.description: Built-in support for orders, multiple payment methods, and payment gateways out of the box.

features.title: Promotions Engine
features.description: Flexible promotions system for discounts, coupon codes, and rule-based pricing adjustments.

features.title: Shipping Configuration
features.description: Configurable shipping methods, rates, and zones to support complex fulfillment needs.

features.title: Extension Ecosystem
features.description: A library of community-maintained extensions adds functionality like subscriptions, marketplaces, and additional payment gateways.

features.title: Headless and API-Ready
features.description: Supports headless commerce architectures through its API, letting teams build custom storefronts on any frontend framework.

features.title: Multi-Store Support
features.description: Can be configured to run multiple storefronts or brands from a single Solidus backend.

features.title: Decentralized Governance
features.description: Maintained by a community-driven governance model with Project Directors and a Core Team rather than a single vendor, reducing platform lock-in risk.

pros: Completely free and open source with no licensing cost
Deep customizability through Rails' native override mechanisms and a Rails engine architecture
Proven at scale by well-known direct-to-consumer brands
Community-driven governance reduces the risk of a single vendor changing direction or shutting down
Strong fit for headless commerce architectures via its API
cons: Requires in-house or agency Ruby on Rails expertise to implement and maintain
No official vendor support line; support comes from the community or paid agency partners
Total cost of ownership can exceed hosted SaaS platforms once developer time is factored in
Smaller ecosystem of extensions and integrations compared to platforms like Shopify

faqs.question: Is Solidus free
faqs.answer: Yes, Solidus is a free, open-source ecommerce framework. There is no license fee, though you pay for hosting, developer time, and any optional paid support.

faqs.question: Who created Solidus
faqs.answer: Solidus was created in 2015 by the consultancy Stembolt as a fork of Spree, aimed at greater stability and backward compatibility.

faqs.question: Who maintains Solidus today
faqs.answer: Solidus is maintained by an open-source community under a decentralized governance model, with the Italian agency Nebulab as the largest code contributor.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Solidus and Spree
faqs.answer: Solidus is a fork of Spree created in 2015 to prioritize stability, easier upgrades, and backward compatibility after Spree's development direction became unstable.

faqs.question: What companies use Solidus
faqs.answer: Well-known brands that have used Solidus include Bonobos, Casper, Away, MeUndies, FLOYD, Maisonette, and Ace and Tate.

faqs.question: Does Solidus support headless commerce
faqs.answer: Yes, Solidus exposes an API that supports headless commerce architectures, allowing teams to build a custom frontend on any framework.

faqs.question: Do I need Ruby on Rails experience to use Solidus
faqs.answer: Yes, Solidus is built as a Ruby on Rails engine, so implementing and customizing it requires Rails development expertise.

use_cases.title: Direct-to-Consumer Online Stores
use_cases.description: Fashion, home goods, and consumer brands build fully customized online stores on Solidus to avoid the constraints of hosted SaaS platforms.

use_cases.title: Headless Commerce Backends
use_cases.description: Engineering teams use Solidus as an API-first backend paired with a custom frontend built in React or another framework.

use_cases.title: Marketplace and Multi-Store Platforms
use_cases.description: Companies running multiple brands or a marketplace configure Solidus to manage several storefronts from a single backend.

features: Full source code under open-source license
Community support via GitHub and Slack
Implementation services from Solidus partner agencies like Nebulab

enrichment.short_description: SonarQube is a self-managed static code analysis platform by SonarSource that continuously checks code quality, bugs, vulnerabilities, and code smells across 30-plus languages.
enrichment.seo_title: SonarQube Pricing and Plans: Server Editions Explained
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SonarQube is a self-managed code quality and security platform by SonarSource. Compare Community, Developer, Enterprise, and Data Center pricing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SonarQube, now marketed as SonarQube Server, is a self-managed static code analysis platform built by SonarSource, a company founded in 2008 and headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland, with additional offices including Austin, Texas. SonarQube continuously scans source code for bugs, vulnerabilities, security hotspots, and code smells across more than 30 programming languages and frameworks, integrating into CI/CD pipelines and pull request workflows so issues are caught before code reaches production. A free, open-source Community Edition is available for individual developers and small teams who self-host the server. Paid editions unlock additional capability and scale: Developer Edition starts around 750 dollars per year for codebases around 100,000 lines of code and adds features like secrets detection and AI-generated code detection; Enterprise Edition, priced by contacting sales, adds portfolio management, unlimited DevOps platform integrations, and AI CodeFix for larger codebases starting around 1 million lines of code; and Data Center Edition adds high availability, horizontal scalability, and Kubernetes autoscaling for the largest, most distributed engineering organizations. SonarQube analyzes hundreds of billions of lines of code daily across its user base and is widely used by engineering and DevSecOps teams that require an on-premises or self-hosted code quality and security solution rather than a cloud SaaS product, for which SonarSource separately offers SonarQube Cloud.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SonarQube
paragraphs: SonarQube, also called SonarQube Server, is a self-managed static code analysis platform from SonarSource that scans source code for bugs, vulnerabilities, security hotspots, and code smells, helping engineering teams maintain code quality and security as part of their development workflow.

SonarSource was founded in 2008 and is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland, with additional offices in Austin, Texas, Bochum, Germany, London, Singapore, and Tokyo. SonarQube is deployed on-premises or in an organization's own cloud infrastructure, distinguishing it from SonarQube Cloud, SonarSource's separately priced hosted SaaS product.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SonarQube integrates directly into CI/CD pipelines and pull request workflows, automatically flagging bugs, vulnerabilities, and code smells across more than 30 languages and frameworks before code merges, with quality gates that can block a build if standards are not met.

Higher editions add capabilities such as secrets detection across hundreds of patterns, AI-generated code detection, AI CodeFix for automated remediation suggestions, portfolio-level reporting across many projects, and compliance support for standards like MISRA C++.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SonarQube offers a free, open-source Community Edition for self-hosted use by individual developers and small teams. Paid editions are licensed per instance per year based on lines of code: Developer Edition starts around 750 dollars annually for around 100,000 lines of code, while Enterprise and Data Center Editions require contacting sales and are aimed at codebases of 1 million lines of code and above.

Because pricing scales with the size of the codebase being analyzed rather than the number of users, larger organizations should budget based on total lines of code across the projects they plan to scan, not headcount.

features.title: Static Code Analysis
features.description: Scans source code across more than 30 languages and frameworks for bugs, vulnerabilities, and code smells.

features.title: Quality Gates
features.description: Blocks a build or pull request from merging if code does not meet configurable quality and security thresholds.

features.title: Secrets Detection
features.description: Detects hardcoded secrets and credentials across hundreds of known patterns in the Developer Edition and above.

features.title: AI-Generated Code Detection
features.description: Identifies AI-generated code in a codebase to help teams apply appropriate review standards.

features.title: AI CodeFix
features.description: Enterprise Edition includes AI-powered fix suggestions for flagged issues to speed up remediation.

features.title: CI/CD and DevOps Integration
features.description: Integrates with popular CI/CD tools and DevOps platforms like GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps, and Bitbucket.

features.title: Portfolio Management
features.description: Enterprise Edition adds portfolio-level dashboards for tracking code quality across many projects and teams.

features.title: High Availability and Scalability
features.description: Data Center Edition adds component redundancy, horizontal scalability, and Kubernetes autoscaling for large distributed deployments.

pros: Free Community Edition provides real static analysis value at no cost for individuals and small teams
Supports a wide range of languages and integrates with most major CI/CD and DevOps platforms
Self-hosted deployment gives full control over source code and data residency
Scales from small teams to large enterprises with Data Center Edition's high availability features
Backed by SonarSource, an established company founded in 2008 with hundreds of employees
cons: Enterprise and Data Center Edition pricing requires contacting sales rather than transparent self-serve pricing
Self-hosting requires infrastructure, installation, and ongoing maintenance effort
Cost scales with lines of code, which can become expensive for very large codebases
Some advanced features like AI CodeFix and portfolio management are limited to higher-tier editions

faqs.question: Is SonarQube free
faqs.answer: SonarQube's Community Edition is free and open source for self-hosted use. Paid Developer, Enterprise, and Data Center editions add features and scale, starting around 750 dollars per year.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SonarQube and SonarQube Cloud
faqs.answer: SonarQube (SonarQube Server) is a self-managed product you host yourself, while SonarQube Cloud, formerly SonarCloud, is SonarSource's hosted SaaS version. Both are made by SonarSource but priced and deployed differently.

faqs.question: What languages does SonarQube support
faqs.answer: SonarQube supports more than 30 programming languages and frameworks in its Developer Edition, with additional languages like COBOL and ABAP available in Enterprise Edition.

faqs.question: Who makes SonarQube
faqs.answer: SonarQube is made by SonarSource, a company founded in 2008 and headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

faqs.question: How is SonarQube priced
faqs.answer: SonarQube is priced per instance per year based on the number of lines of code analyzed, not the number of users.

faqs.question: Does SonarQube integrate with CI/CD pipelines
faqs.answer: Yes, SonarQube integrates with popular CI/CD tools and DevOps platforms, and can block pull requests or builds that fail configured quality gates.

faqs.question: What is Data Center Edition for
faqs.answer: Data Center Edition is designed for large, distributed engineering organizations that need high availability, horizontal scalability, and Kubernetes autoscaling for analyzing very large codebases.

use_cases.title: Self-Hosted Code Quality Gates
use_cases.description: Engineering teams add SonarQube to their CI/CD pipeline to block merges that introduce bugs, vulnerabilities, or excessive code smells.

use_cases.title: Enterprise Security and Compliance
use_cases.description: Large organizations use Enterprise or Data Center Edition to track code quality and security across many projects and enforce compliance standards like MISRA.

use_cases.title: Regulated or Air-Gapped Environments
use_cases.description: Organizations that cannot use cloud SaaS tools deploy SonarQube on-premises to keep source code analysis entirely within their own infrastructure.

features: Supports popular languages including Java, JavaScript, Python, and C#

pricing_plans.billing_cycle: Annual, per instance based on lines of code
Annual, per instance based on lines of code
Annual, per instance based on lines of code

enrichment.short_description: SonarQube Cloud, formerly SonarCloud, is SonarSource's hosted SaaS code quality and security platform, integrating directly with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure DevOps.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (up to 50k lines of code); Team plan from $34/month
enrichment.seo_title: SonarQube Cloud Pricing: Free, Team, and Enterprise
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SonarQube Cloud, formerly SonarCloud, is SonarSource's hosted code quality SaaS. Compare Free, Team, and Enterprise pricing plans and features.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SonarQube Cloud, formerly known as SonarCloud, is the hosted SaaS code quality and security product from SonarSource, the company founded in 2008 and headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland, that also makes the self-managed SonarQube Server product. Unlike SonarQube Server, which organizations install and run on their own infrastructure, SonarQube Cloud is a fully managed service that connects directly to source code hosts like GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure DevOps, automatically analyzing pull requests and branches without requiring customers to manage servers. SonarQube Cloud offers a free tier for exploring the product with private projects up to 50,000 lines of code, plus a separate free plan for open-source projects, supporting 30-plus languages, branch analysis, and pull request decoration. The paid Team plan starts around 34 dollars per month for up to 100,000 lines of code, with pricing scaling in increments up to 1.9 million lines of code, billed monthly or annually. The Enterprise plan, available only on an annual billing cycle and priced based on lines of code with no upper limit, adds single sign-on via SAML and SCIM, security and regulatory reports, customizable dashboards, enterprise hierarchy and portfolio creation, audit logs, IP allow lists, and support for enterprise languages such as COBOL and ABAP. SonarQube Cloud is best suited for teams that want code quality and security scanning without operating their own SonarQube server infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SonarQube Cloud
paragraphs: SonarQube Cloud, formerly called SonarCloud, is the fully managed, hosted version of SonarSource's code quality and security analysis platform. Instead of installing and maintaining a server, teams connect their GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or Azure DevOps repositories directly to SonarQube Cloud, and it automatically analyzes commits, branches, and pull requests.

SonarQube Cloud is built by SonarSource, founded in 2008 and headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland. It is a separate product from SonarQube Server, the self-managed, on-premises version, with its own pricing model based on lines of code analyzed rather than per-instance annual licensing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SonarQube Cloud automatically decorates pull requests with inline quality and security feedback, supports branch analysis, and covers more than 30 programming languages and frameworks, all without requiring customers to provision or maintain analysis server infrastructure.

The Enterprise plan adds capabilities aimed at larger organizations, including single sign-on via SAML and SCIM, security and regulatory compliance reports, customizable dashboards, an enterprise hierarchy for organizing many projects into portfolios, audit logs, IP allow lists, and support for enterprise languages such as COBOL, ABAP, JCL, RPG, PL/I, and Apex.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SonarQube Cloud's Free plan supports private projects up to 50,000 lines of code, with a separate free plan available for open-source projects. The Team plan starts around 34 dollars per month for up to 100,000 lines of code, with additional lines-of-code increments available up to 1.9 million, billed monthly or annually.

The Enterprise plan is billed annually only, priced based on total lines of code with no upper limit, and requires contacting SonarSource sales for a quote, since pricing depends on codebase size and specific enterprise feature requirements.

features.title: Fully Managed SaaS Analysis
features.description: No servers to install or maintain; SonarQube Cloud connects directly to your source code host and runs analysis automatically.

features.title: Pull Request Decoration
features.description: Adds inline quality and security feedback directly on pull requests in GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure DevOps.

features.title: Branch Analysis
features.description: Analyzes every branch, not just the main branch, to catch issues earlier in the development process.

features.title: 30-Plus Language Support
features.description: Covers more than 30 programming languages and frameworks across the Free and Team plans.

features.title: Open Source Plan
features.description: Offers a dedicated free plan for public open-source repositories, separate from the private-project free tier.

features.title: Enterprise SSO and Compliance
features.description: Enterprise plan adds SAML and SCIM single sign-on, audit logs, IP allow lists, and security and regulatory reports.

features.title: Portfolio and Enterprise Hierarchy
features.description: Enterprise plan lets large organizations group many projects into portfolios for aggregated reporting.

features.title: Enterprise Language Support
features.description: Enterprise plan adds support for languages like COBOL, ABAP, JCL, RPG, PL/I, and Apex used in legacy and enterprise systems.

pros: No infrastructure to manage since it is a fully hosted SaaS product
Free tier is generous enough for small private projects and fully free for open-source repositories
Deep, native integration with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure DevOps pull request workflows
Scales cleanly from individual developers to large enterprises via lines-of-code-based pricing
Backed by SonarSource, an established vendor with a long track record in code quality tooling
cons: Pricing scales with lines of code, which can become expensive for very large codebases
Enterprise plan requires annual billing and a custom sales quote rather than transparent pricing
Code is analyzed on SonarSource's cloud infrastructure, which may not suit organizations with strict data residency requirements
Free tier's 50,000 line limit for private projects is easy to outgrow for actively developed codebases

faqs.question: Is SonarQube Cloud the same as SonarCloud
faqs.answer: Yes, SonarQube Cloud is the renamed version of SonarCloud, SonarSource's hosted SaaS code quality and security product.

faqs.question: Is SonarQube Cloud free
faqs.answer: Yes, SonarQube Cloud offers a free tier for private projects up to 50,000 lines of code, and a separate free plan for open-source projects.

faqs.question: How much does the SonarQube Cloud Team plan cost
faqs.answer: The Team plan starts around 34 dollars per month for up to 100,000 lines of code, with pricing increasing in tiers up to 1.9 million lines of code.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SonarQube Cloud and SonarQube Server
faqs.answer: SonarQube Cloud is a fully managed SaaS product hosted by SonarSource, while SonarQube Server, SonarQube, is self-managed software you install and run on your own infrastructure. Both are made by SonarSource but priced and deployed differently.

faqs.question: Does SonarQube Cloud support pull request analysis
faqs.answer: Yes, SonarQube Cloud automatically decorates pull requests with inline quality and security feedback in GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure DevOps.

faqs.question: Does SonarQube Cloud offer single sign-on
faqs.answer: Yes, single sign-on via SAML and SCIM is available on the Enterprise plan.

faqs.question: Who makes SonarQube Cloud
faqs.answer: SonarQube Cloud is made by SonarSource, a company founded in 2008 and headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

use_cases.title: SaaS Pull Request Quality Checks
use_cases.description: Development teams connect SonarQube Cloud to their GitHub or GitLab repositories to automatically check every pull request for bugs and vulnerabilities.

use_cases.title: Open Source Project Analysis
use_cases.description: Open-source maintainers use SonarQube Cloud's free open-source plan to keep public repositories free of quality and security issues.

use_cases.title: Enterprise Portfolio Code Quality
use_cases.description: Large enterprises use the Enterprise plan to track code quality and security across hundreds of projects organized into portfolios, with SSO and audit logging.

features: Private projects up to 50,000 lines of code
Unlimited lines of code for public open-source repositories
Full pull request and branch analysis
Up to 100,000 lines of code at entry pricing, scaling to 1.9 million LOC

pricing_plans.price: Custom pricing based on lines of code
features: No upper limit on lines of code
Single sign-on via SAML and SCIM
Audit logs and IP allow lists
Enterprise languages including COBOL, ABAP, JCL, RPG, PL/I, and Apex
